stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Confined masonry begins with proper site preparation and foundation planning.
stages.explanation: Confined masonry begins with proper site preparation and foundation planning.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. Grid lines and tie-column positions are marked on site.
stages.explanation: Setting out establishes structural geometry and confinement locations.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Foundation trenches are excavated to stable bearing strata.
stages.explanation: Excavation reaches stable bearing strata capable of supporting structural loads.

stages.title: PCC Layer
stages.narration: Stage 4. Plain cement concrete is placed to form a level working base.
stages.explanation: PCC provides a clean and stable base for structural concrete.

stages.title: RCC Footings
stages.narration: Stage 5. Reinforced concrete footings are cast with rebar cages, stirrups, and vibrated concrete.
stages.explanation: Footings distribute building loads safely into the supporting soil.

stages.title: Plinth Beam
stages.narration: Stage 6. The plinth beam is cast to tie footings and support tie-columns and walls.
stages.explanation: The plinth beam ties the structure together and controls differential settlement.

stages.title: Tie Column Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 7. Vertical reinforcement cages for tie-columns are erected at corners and wall intersections.
stages.explanation: Tie columns are the primary confinement elements that improve seismic performance.

stages.title: Concrete Block Wall Construction
stages.narration: Stage 8. Concrete blocks are laid course-by-course in mortar. Tie-column cavities remain open for later casting.
stages.explanation: Masonry walls provide gravity load support while confinement improves seismic behavior.

stages.title: Casting of Tie Columns
stages.narration: Stage 9. Reinforced concrete tie columns are now being cast. These elements confine the masonry walls and significantly improve seismic resistance by preventing brittle failure.
stages.explanation: Tie columns confine masonry and prevent brittle wall failure.

stages.title: Window and Door Openings
stages.narration: Stage 10. Door and window frames are installed with reinforcement around openings.
stages.explanation: Openings create stress concentrations and require proper confinement.

stages.title: Lintel Band
stages.narration: Stage 11. Continuous lintel band concrete is cast around the structure.
stages.explanation: Lintel bands connect walls and improve earthquake resistance.

stages.title: Tie Beam System
stages.narration: Stage 12. Horizontal tie beams connect all tie-columns forming a continuous load path.
stages.explanation: Tie beams create a continuous load path throughout the structure.

stages.title: Roof Band
stages.narration: Stage 13. Roof-level ring beam is cast to create box action during seismic events.
stages.explanation: Roof bands create box action behavior during seismic events.

stages.title: RCC Roof Slab
stages.narration: Stage 14. Roof slab formwork, reinforcement, concrete placement, and curing complete the structural frame.
stages.explanation: The roof slab distributes loads and ties the structure together.

stages.title: Finishing Works
stages.narration: Stage 15. Plaster, doors, windows, floor finish, and painting are completed.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. Confined concrete block masonry provides ductile earthquake-resistant performance through tie-columns and continuous bands.
stages.explanation: Fully confined masonry house with integrated RC frame elements.

foundation.title: RCC Footing
foundation.loadDistribution: Spreads column and wall reactions over bearing area.
foundation.settlementPrevention: Stiff plinth beam reduces differential movement.

tie_column.title: Tie Column
tie_column.confinementAction: RC column encases masonry panel edges preventing out-of-plane failure.
tie_column.seismicRole: Provides ductility and holds walls in compression during shaking.
tie_column.ductility: Proper detailing with ties allows controlled deformation.

tie_beam.title: Tie Beam
tie_beam.forceRedistribution: Connects tie-columns horizontally distributing inertia forces.
tie_beam.continuity: Creates closed frame at plinth, lintel, and roof levels.

concrete_block_wall.title: Concrete Block Wall
concrete_block_wall.compressionBehavior: Bearing walls work primarily in compression under gravity loads.
concrete_block_wall.loadTransfer: Loads pass to plinth, bands, and tie-columns.

lintel_band.title: Lintel Band
lintel_band.wallIntegration: Continuous RC band at opening heads ties wall panels.
lintel_band.seismicProtection: Prevents separation of walls at lintel level during earthquakes.

roof_band.title: Roof Band
roof_band.boxAction: Ring beam with roof slab creates rigid diaphragm.
roof_band.earthquakePerformance: Forces shared among all walls in plan.

roof_slab.title: RCC Roof Slab
roof_slab.reinforcementLayout: Main and distribution bars per span direction.
roof_slab.loadPath: Slab → roof band → tie columns → footings → soil
